issue: The gloss '4.POSS=elder.brother-POSS exist' uses 'POSS' for both the possessive prefix and the affiliative suffix, but the glossing standard typically distinguishes these; the suffix should be glossed as -AFF or similar to match the concept of 'affiliative form'.
Same issue as in line 14: the suffix -ha on 'sa' is an affiliative suffix and should not be glossed as 'POSS' on both the prefix and the suffix; this leads to ambiguity in morpheme alignment.
The gloss 'child-POSS' repeats the same issue: the suffix -ho is an affiliative marker and should be glossed distinctly from the possessive prefix.

The compound 'ramat-kor' is glossed as 'soul-have', but the morpheme-by-morpheme segmentation implies 'ramat-kor' is a single unit; the gloss should reflect incorporation, possibly as 'soul-HAVE' or 'soul-holding'.
The 'source' column in the table uses informal citation format (author year: page) instead of the standard <Ref> markup used elsewhere in the chapter, creating inconsistency in citation style.
suggestion: Replace with <Ref k="nakagawa2024" p="323" /> and <Ref k="bugaeva2012" p="496" />

issue: Same issue as line 253 — source is given in plain text instead of <Ref> markup, breaking citation consistency.
The plural existential is spelled 'okay' in line 13 but 'oka' in line 185; this variation should be noted or standardized, as it may reflect dialectal or orthographic inconsistency.
suggestion: Add a note on variation or standardize to 'oka' if preferred; otherwise mark as ~okay

issue: The spelling 'okay' and 'oka' are presented as variants, but elsewhere only 'okay' is used; this should be consistently marked throughout the chapter.
suggestion: Ensure all instances reflect the variant form: okay~oka

issue: The free translation is given as 'was pregnant with (lit. hold-belly-have, one-exists)' — the literal translation is garbled and does not match the morpheme breakdown.
suggestion: Revise literal translation to '3POSS=belly-have CONJ exist=4INTR' or similar

issue: The example includes '=an' as a suffix on the existential verb, but the gloss does not reflect this; the gloss should include the 4th-person intransitive suffix.
